title: Ontology-Driven Mixture-of-Domain Documentation: A Backbone Approach Enabling Question Answering for Additive Construction
task: 本体驱动的跨领域知识文档化与知识图谱构建
contribution: 提出基于本体驱动的混合领域(MoD)文档化方法，以BFO为基础本体构建模块化本体体系（涵盖混凝土材料、建筑设计和打印系统），实现增材建造跨领域知识的文档化、知识图谱构建，并为未来基于LLM的知识图谱问答(KGQA)奠定基础
pdfFile: Li, C. etal - 2025 - Ontology-Driven Mixture-of-Domain Documentation A Backbone Approach Enabling Question Answering for....pdf
markdownFile: Li, C. etal - 2025 - Ontology-Driven Mixture-of-Domain Documentation A Backbone Approach Enabling Question Answering for....md
abstract: Advanced construction techniques, such as additive manufacturing (AM) and modular construction, offer promising solutions to address labor shortages, reduce CO2 emissions, and enhance material efficiency. Despite their potential, the adoption of these innovative methods is hindered by the construction industry's fragmented expertise. Building Information Modeling (BIM) is frequently suggested to integrate this diverse knowledge, but existing BIM-based approaches lack a robust framework for systematically documenting and retrieving the cross-domain knowledge essential for construction projects. To bridge this gap, this paper presents an ontology-driven methodology for documenting and utilizing expert knowledge, with a focus on AM in construction. Based on a well-founded ontological framework, a set of modular ontologies is formalized for individual domains. Additionally, a prototypical documentation tool is developed to elevate recorded information and BIM models as a knowledge graph. This knowledge graph will interface with advanced large language models (LLMs), enabling effective question answering and knowledge retrieval.
methodology: The approach adopts BFO as a foundational ontology and IOF Core as a mid-level ontology to build a unified ontological framework. Modular ontologies are formalized for concrete composition and properties (CCPO), building design (BDO), printing system (PSO), and additive construction (ACO), along with a modal extension ontology (MEO) and a narrative ontology (MoD). Existing ontologies (BOT, CORA, MSEO, etc.) are aligned and reused via syntactic locality module extraction (SLME). A prototypical documentation tool is developed for BIM-based expert knowledge capture, and SPARQL queries validate the knowledge graph with data from real 3D concrete printing experiments.
results: The proposed ontology suite was validated through use cases formalizing real shotcrete printing experiments from the literature, demonstrating that textual domain narratives can be transcribed into structured RDF graph representations. SPARQL queries successfully retrieved robotic system configurations, material compositions, process parameters, and printing outcomes. The prototypical documentation tool demonstrated feasibility for capturing multidisciplinary expertise on BIM models in a coherent knowledge graph.
taskEn: Ontology-driven cross-domain knowledge documentation and knowledge graph construction
